sectionsUnavailable: No direct Metabolic Health DNA Report sample report has been captured.
Sibling Food Sensitivity and Healthy Nutrition sample rows are used only as observed metabolic-health DNA output-structure evidence.

No calibrated metabolic, glucose, diabetes, insulin-resistance, weight, food-response, micronutrient, supplement, diet-history, symptom, lab, medication, or ancestry applicability model is supplied with this fixture.
reportOverview.plainEnglishMeaning: This run can describe supplied lactose-related marker context and reproduce sibling nutrition sample output structure, but it cannot diagnose metabolic disease, predict diabetes, infer insulin resistance, predict glucose, interpret labs, model BMI or weight, set nutrient targets, recommend diet or supplements, change medications, or guide treatment.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived MCM6/LCT rs4988235 CT call is available for lactase-persistence context. This is not a metabolic diagnosis, glucose prediction, diabetes prediction, insulin-resistance result, BMI or weight result, lab interpretation, diet recommendation, supplement recommendation, medication result, or treatment result.
resultRows.description: Local synthetic fixture row for conservative lactose-related marker context.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This marker can provide lactose-related context, but symptoms, diet history, labs, medications, environment, ancestry, and professional review matter for metabolic-health decisions.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: No calibrated metabolic score, glucose model, diabetes model, insulin-resistance model, weight model, food-response model, micronutrient model, supplement model, diet-history context, symptom context, lab context, medication context, or ancestry applicability metadata is supplied with this fixture.
resultRows.description: Local synthetic fixture row marking the package-specific calibrated model and non-genetic context unavailable.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The report can describe supplied marker context, but it should not make metabolic, glucose, diabetes, insulin-resistance, BMI/weight, lab, diet, supplement, medication, diagnosis, or treatment conclusions without validated evidence.

resultRows.groupTitle: Sibling sample report: Sensitivity results summary
resultRows.item: Alcohol, caffeine, lactose, and gluten summary cards
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample summary cards show Alcohol: Slow Metabolism; Caffeine: Fast Metabolism; Lactose: Likely Sensitive; Gluten: Not Likely Sensitive, with a legend separating likely-sensitive from not-likely-sensitive labels.
resultRows.description: The sample summary page presents the four result cards before detailed substance and sensitivity sections.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The sample summary page presents the four result cards before detailed substance and sensitivity sections. Observed sibling sample-report structure only for Metabolic Health DNA Report formatting. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a direct Metabolic Health DNA Report sample, metabolic disease finding, diabetes-risk estimate, insulin-resistance inference, glucose prediction, HbA1c or lab interpretation, BMI/weight result, nutrient target, diet plan, supplement recommendation, medication action, diagnosis, or treatment guidance.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample introduces alcohol and caffeine as substances whose metabolism can vary with genetic markers, then notes that slower metabolism markers may mean the body takes longer to clear alcohol or caffeine.
resultRows.description: The substances section precedes the detailed Alcohol Metabolism and Caffeine Metabolism pages.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The substances section precedes the detailed Alcohol Metabolism and Caffeine Metabolism pages. Observed sibling sample-report structure only for Metabolic Health DNA Report formatting. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a direct Metabolic Health DNA Report sample, metabolic disease finding, diabetes-risk estimate, insulin-resistance inference, glucose prediction, HbA1c or lab interpretation, BMI/weight result, nutrient target, diet plan, supplement recommendation, medication action, diagnosis, or treatment guidance.

resultRows.item: ADH1C gene table and slow-metabolism result
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Alcohol Metabolism page says the sample genome is more likely to have slow alcohol metabolism. The visible gene table lists ADH1C marker GP000071 genotype TT with slow alcohol metabolism impact, plus ADH1C GP000072 genotype CC and ADH1C GP000073 genotype TT with normal alcohol metabolism impact.
resultRows.description: The page also lists common alcohol sources and a what-can-you-do block about tracking drinks, spacing them out, and drinking water.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The page also lists common alcohol sources and a what-can-you-do block about tracking drinks, spacing them out, and drinking water. resultRows.item: CYP1A2 gene table and fast-metabolism result
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Caffeine Metabolism page says the sample genome is more likely to have fast caffeine metabolism. The visible gene table lists CYP1A2 marker GP000071 genotype TT with fast caffeine metabolism impact.
resultRows.description: The page lists common caffeine sources such as coffee, tea, soft drinks, energy drinks, chocolate, certain medications, and yerba mate, plus a sleep-timing action block.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The page lists common caffeine sources such as coffee, tea, soft drinks, energy drinks, chocolate, certain medications, and yerba mate, plus a sleep-timing action block. resultRows.item: Food sensitivity versus food allergy explanation
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Sensitivities section distinguishes food sensitivity from food allergy, explains that sensitivities can involve difficulty digesting components such as lactose or gluten, and names non-genetic contributors such as gut microbiome imbalance, gastrointestinal disease, stress, age, additives, sleep, and physical inactivity.
resultRows.description: The section explicitly says genetics can play a role but do not work in isolation.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The section explicitly says genetics can play a role but do not work in isolation. resultRows.item: HLA-DQA1 and HLA-DQ8 gene table with not-likely-sensitive result
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Gluten Sensitivity page says the sample genome is not likely sensitive to gluten. The visible gene table lists HLA-DQA1 marker GP000071 genotype TT and HLA-DQ8 marker GP000072 genotype CC, both with not-likely-sensitive-to-gluten impact.
resultRows.description: The page lists common gluten sources and includes a what-can-you-do block about a temporary gluten-removal trial.

metabolicDnaContext: Use this report as educational nutrition-genetics context, not as a metabolic diagnosis or treatment guide.
Bring symptoms, diet history, labs, medications, weight questions, and health goals to qualified professionals for metabolic-health decisions.
Do not change foods, supplements, medications, or treatment based on genotype or sibling sample rows alone.

missingInputs: No direct Metabolic Health DNA Report sample report has been captured.

No calibrated metabolic, glucose, diabetes, insulin-resistance, weight, food-response, micronutrient, supplement, diet-history, symptom, lab, medication, or ancestry-applicability model is supplied with this fixture.
Symptoms, food history, diet history, labs, medications, medical history, weight history, ancestry applicability, and professional assessment are not represented in this fixture.
uncertainty: Metabolic health, glucose, weight, nutrient status, symptoms, and food response depend on genetics plus diet history, labs, medical history, medications, environment, ancestry, and professional review.

A single marker and sibling sample rows should not be used to diagnose metabolic disease, predict diabetes, infer insulin resistance, interpret labs, prescribe diet or supplement changes, change medications, or guide treatment.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.

The report is educational and does not diagnose metabolic disease, diagnose diabetes, predict diabetes risk, infer insulin resistance, predict glucose, interpret labs, prescribe diets, recommend supplements, change medications, predict weight outcomes, or provide treatment guidance.
Sibling sample rows are output-structure examples only and do not claim full detail-page parity.
